prompt: Edit this studio worktable photo into a caught-in-the-act moment. Add a maker's bare hands entering frame, mid-cut with brass tailor's shears slicing through the floral printed cotton, a faint tailor's-chalk line marked on the fabric, a scatter of dressmaking pins and a few loose threads on the wood, a paper pattern piece with pencil marks half-tucked under the cloth. Slight natural motion in the cutting hand. Hands only, no face. Keep the warm honey Mediterranean grade and soft directional window daylight of the source. Natural unfinished-wood worktable. The hero fabric is Me + Lia's small blue-and-white floral printed cotton. Make it look like a real, candid photograph of a working dressmaking atelier mid-task, not a styled stock flatlay. 35mm film look, gentle natural grain, realistic shallow depth of field, authentic asymmetry and imperfection. No text, no logos, no watermarks. Portrait framing.
